title: Complete DID resolution algorithm with revocation and delegation discovery

artifacts.description: Extend existing DID method spec with full CRUD lifecycle: Create (enrollment tx → DID Document), Read (resolve commitment → on-chain lookup + Merkle inclusion), Update (immutable — explicit non-operation), Deactivate (revocation nullifier check). Add resolution algorithm pseudocode, error codes (STALE_ROOT, REVOKED_IDENTITY, NOT_FOUND, INVALID_COMMITMENT), and delegationService endpoint definition.
Standalone resolution algorithm document: input/output types, step-by-step resolver logic, caching policy for Merkle roots, conformance requirements per W3C DID Core §7.

Concrete JSON-LD DID Document examples for each lifecycle operation: freshly enrolled human identity, resolved agent credential DID, deactivated (revoked) identity with nullifier proof, and DID Document with delegationService endpoint.
Error code registry: invalidDid, notFound, deactivated (maps to revocationNullifierFound), representationNotSupported, staleRoot (Merkle root lag > threshold), delegationChainBroken.

TypeScript DID resolver implementing the algorithm: resolveHuman(commitment) and resolveAgent(credentialHash) returning W3C DID Resolution Result objects, with on-chain EnrollmentRegistry lookups, nullifier checks, and delegationService URL construction.
DID Document type definitions: DidDocument, VerificationMethod (Ed25519 + BLS12-381), ServiceEndpoint, DelegationService, ResolutionMetadata, DocumentMetadata.

On-chain registry emitting EnrollmentRegistered and IdentityDeactivated events with indexed commitment fields to support resolution queries. Exposes currentMerkleRoot() and isNullifierRevoked(bytes32) view functions.

Conformance test suite validating the resolver against all W3C DID Core resolution algorithm requirements: happy path, stale-root error, revoked identity error, delegation chain discovery, and representation negotiation.
Unit tests for the TypeScript resolver: mock EnrollmentRegistry, nullifier state, and Merkle inclusion proofs. Covers all error code paths.

steps: Audit existing spec/did-method-bolyra.md to inventory what syntax and DID Document structure is already defined; note gaps in CRUD lifecycle coverage
Define the canonical DID syntax: did:bolyra:<network>:<commitment-hash> for humans, did:bolyra:<network>:agent:<credential-hash> for agents

Specify Create operation: map enrollment transaction (EnrollmentRegistry.enroll(commitment)) to DID Document with Ed25519 verificationMethod derived from commitment, plus Merkle inclusion proof as documentMetadata

Specify Read (resolve) operation: (1) parse DID → extract commitment/credential hash, (2) query EnrollmentRegistry for EnrollmentRegistered event, (3) verify current Merkle inclusion via inclusion proof or on-chain root, (4) check nullifier registry for deactivation, (5) construct DID Document with current verificationMethods and serviceEndpoints
Specify Update operation explicitly as NOT SUPPORTED with rationale: commitments are cryptographic commitments, immutable by design; key rotation requires new enrollment and DID

Specify Deactivate operation: submitting a valid revocation nullifier to NullifierRegistry marks the DID as deactivated; resolver MUST return deactivated=true in documentMetadata and error code 'deactivated'

Define delegationService endpoint schema: type='BolyraD elegationChain', serviceEndpoint pointing to delegation discovery API with did parameter; describe how resolver populates this from on-chain delegation events
Define error codes and map to W3C DID resolution error vocabulary: invalidDid, notFound, deactivated, staleRoot (configurable lag threshold), delegationChainBroken, representationNotSupported
Write concrete JSON-LD examples for all operations and embed in spec/did-resolution-examples.jsonld with @context referencing did-bolyra context document
Implement DID Document types in sdk/src/did/types.ts aligned with W3C DID Core data model

Implement resolver in sdk/src/did/resolver.ts: resolveHuman and resolveAgent functions, ethers.js queries against DIDRegistry and NullifierRegistry, Merkle inclusion verification via snarkjs witness check

Add DIDRegistry.sol with EnrollmentRegistered(bytes32 indexed commitment, address enrollee, uint256 timestamp) and IdentityDeactivated(bytes32 indexed nullifier) events plus view functions currentMerkleRoot() and isNullifierRevoked(bytes32)
Write conformance test suite covering all W3C DID Core §7 resolution algorithm steps
Write SDK unit tests mocking on-chain state for all error paths
Update spec/did-method-bolyra.md to reference the new resolution algorithm document and examples
Cross-reference FORMAL-PROPERTIES.md to ensure resolver's nullifier check aligns with circuit-enforced nullifier domain separation
dependencies: circuits/src/HumanUniqueness.circom (nullifierHash output used as deactivation key)
circuits/src/AgentPolicy.circom (credentialHash used as agent DID identifier)
W3C DID Resolution spec (resolutionMetadata + documentMetadata types)
snarkjs (Merkle inclusion witness verification in resolver)
ethers.js v6 (on-chain event and view function queries)
